title: 'The Fantastic Four: First Steps' Is Here. Plus: Our Fall Film Festival Preview.
format: Sean and Amanda run down the just-announced Venice, TIFF, Telluride, and New York lineups and gauge which titles turn into the fall awards race, then review Marvel's 'The Fantastic Four: First Steps' — a movie Amanda hated and Sean found baffling and limp. A Starbucks-sponsored 'Summer Stars' interlude sorts the season's standout performances.

segments.heading: The fall film festival preview
segments.note: Venice, TIFF, Telluride, and New York lineups

films.title: The Smashing Machine
films.note: Benny Safdie's Dwayne Johnson MMA drama, a Venice world premiere and a likely TIFF play. Half of A24's Safdie double this fall.

films.title: Frankenstein
films.note: Guillermo del Toro's retelling of the Mary Shelley novel for Netflix, at Venice and TIFF. Sean names it a live Best Picture question mark.

films.title: After the Hunt
films.note: Luca Guadagnino and Julia Roberts on campus sexual assault, playing out of competition at Venice by Luca's request and opening the New York Film Festival — its North American premiere, which Sean thinks blocks a Telluride jump.

films.title: Bugonia
films.note: Yorgos Lanthimos and Emma Stone again, at Venice. Sean is hoping it makes the Telluride jump, as 'Poor Things' once did.

films.title: Jay Kelly
films.note: Noah Baumbach's Netflix film about a fading movie star, with George Clooney and Adam Sandler. One of the titles Sean needs at Telluride to feel calm.

films.title: A House of Dynamite
films.note: Kathryn Bigelow's return, via Netflix, at Venice.

films.title: No Other Choice
films.note: Park Chan-wook's new film; Sean says the teaser looks phenomenal and he's still sending emails about it.

films.title: Father, Mother, Sister, Brother
films.note: Jim Jarmusch's reportedly anthology film, at Venice and set as the New York Film Festival centerpiece — the anthology structure being much to Amanda's chagrin.

films.title: The Testament of Ann Lee
films.note: Mona Fastvold's new directorial effort, at Venice and TIFF. Filed among Sean's awards-list wildcards.

films.title: The Wizard of the Kremlin
films.note: Olivier Assayas's film about Vladimir Putin (Jude Law) and an imagined attaché (Paul Dano). Could be silly, could be great.

films.title: Dead Man's Wire
films.note: Gus Van Sant's first movie in seven or eight years, at Venice and TIFF.

films.title: Scarlet
films.note: Mamoru Hosoda's animated film, which Sean calls the front-runner for best animated feature of 2025.

films.title: The Christophers
films.note: Steven Soderbergh's TIFF entry, among the titles they'd already expected there.

films.title: Rental Family
films.note: A TIFF play they'd penciled in.

films.title: Hedda
films.note: Expected at TIFF.

films.title: Wake Up Dead Man
films.note: Rian Johnson's third Knives Out mystery for Netflix, expected at TIFF. Part of what Sean calls a strong Netflix year.

films.title: Roofman
films.note: Expected at TIFF.

films.title: Hamnet
films.note: Chloé Zhao's film, announced as a TIFF non-world premiere and, given Zhao's history there, almost certain to launch at Telluride first.

films.title: Ballad of a Small Player
films.note: The Netflix title that missed Venice; Sean expects it at Telluride, where Edward Berger had 'Conclave' last year.

films.title: It Was Just an Accident
films.note: Jafar Panahi's Palme d'Or winner, a Cannes holdover now at TIFF and a live contender.

films.title: Nouvelle Vague
films.note: Richard Linklater's Cannes holdover, playing TIFF.

films.title: The Secret Agent
films.note: A Cannes holdover in the awards conversation, at TIFF.

films.title: Sentimental Value
films.note: Joachim Trier's Cannes title, a holdover at TIFF and one of the Cannes winners Sean rates as likely to stick.

films.title: Springsteen: Deliver Me From Nowhere
films.note: Scott Cooper's Springsteen film with Jeremy Allen White; Sean floats it as a safe Telluride landing spot and an Oscar-campaign launch.

films.title: Ella McKay
films.note: James L. Brooks's December release, which could skip the circuit but would fit Telluride.

films.title: Is This Thing On?
films.note: Bradley Cooper's undated film, which Amanda thinks lands this year; Sean doubts a Telluride slot.

films.title: Anemone
films.note: Ronan Day-Lewis's debut, notable for bringing Daniel Day-Lewis out of retirement. Sean would love to see it in Colorado but doubts it plays.

films.title: Marty Supreme
films.note: Josh Safdie's Christmas release, being held out of the festivals. The other half of A24's Safdie double.

films.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey
films.note: The Margot Robbie / Colin Farrell film, moved to September but absent from every festival, which reads to Amanda as a warning sign.

films.title: The History of Sound
films.note: Out right after the fall festivals, currently unpegged to any of them.

films.title: The Mastermind
films.note: Kelly Reichardt's Cannes premiere, not yet listed at a fall festival.

films.title: Die My Love
films.note: The Jennifer Lawrence acquisition, out in November; Sean bets it turns up at Telluride.

films.title: The Way of the Wind
films.note: Terrence Malick's long-in-the-edit film, which missed Venice again. Just keep chiseling, brother.

films.title: The Rivals of Amziah King
films.note: A South by Southwest winner with no distribution yet; Sean wonders if it surfaces at a fall festival on the strength of its reviews.

films.title: Train Dreams
films.note: The Netflix logging drama, one of Sean's favorite films of the year, that just got a trailer.

films.title: Wicked for Good
films.note: A studio tentpole in the Best Picture running that won't play a festival.

films.title: Avatar: Fire and Ash
films.note: The other studio contender skipping the circuit. The trailer, shown before 'Fantastic Four,' left Sean nonplussed — 'not a lot of fire or ash.'

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: The Paul Thomas Anderson film Warner Bros. is keeping out of Venice, likely to just drop in theaters in September. One of Sean's 25 located contenders.

films.title: Sinners
films.note: A studio big swing already on Sean's list of located Best Picture contenders.

films.title: The Drama
films.note: Kristoffer Borgli's film with Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, flagged as probably a 2026 release.

films.title: Mother Mary
films.note: David Lowery's film with Anne Hathaway, likely 2026.

films.title: I Want Your Sex
films.note: Gregg Araki's film with Charli XCX, probably a 2026 title.

segments.heading: 'The Fantastic Four: First Steps'
segments.note: Sean's birthday movie, and a limp one

films.title: The Fantastic 4: First Steps
films.note: Matt Shakman directs Pedro Pascal, Vanessa Kirby, Joseph Quinn, Ebon Moss-Bachrach, Julia Garner, and Ralph Ineson's Galactus. Amanda flat-out hated it; Sean found it juiceless and baffling, with weak effects and Pascal badly miscast as a charmless Reed Richards. Both credit the in-media-res newsreel open and the retro production design, and both are worn down by the CGI and the 'super mama saves the day' resolution.

films.title: Fantastic Four
films.note: The Ioan Gruffudd / Jessica Alba / Chris Evans version that producer Jack adored as a kid. Sean thought it trash in college, but the table agrees Evans has chemistry and charisma that Joseph Quinn's Johnny Storm lacks.

films.title: Fantastic Four
films.note: The Josh Trank version Sean never saw, remembered mainly for a Galactus rendered as a cloud.

films.title: The Incredibles
films.note: Sean's closing argument: it's the Fantastic Four but good, and it has obviated these characters — Violet's force field and invisibility, Jack-Jack's flames, all of it, folded into one family.

films.title: Incredibles 2
films.note: The second half of the Pixar homage the hosts say makes 'Fantastic Four' a harder sell.

films.title: Cut Bank
films.note: Matt Shakman's only prior feature, an A24 movie from a decade ago, before he directed every episode of 'WandaVision.'

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
films.note: The comparison Amanda reaches for on production design — good textures, in a movie she otherwise didn't love.

films.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
films.note: Cited for the Charlize Theron end-credits tease and for the Scarlet Witch turning villain — the kind of marketing hook this movie's stinger doesn't earn.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: The Harry Styles post-credits reveal, another example of a stinger that Sean's 'dumb lizard brain' falls for.

films.title: Thunderbolts
films.note: Its end-credits rocket ship with a four on it primed the crossover; Florence Pugh and Lewis Pullman are held up as actors who stepped up in a Marvel movie the way this cast doesn't.

films.title: Avengers: Infinity War
films.note: Half of the payoff Sean says worked because Marvel spent ten years building Thanos — the opposite of this movie's cold start.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: The conclusion Sean calls the real work of art, the push toward the ending rather than any single film.

films.title: The Flash
films.note: The bar for babies-in-peril: nothing here is as bad as flinging infants out of hospital windows.

films.title: Superman
films.note: The two-weeks-apart counterpoint. Sean says Gunn plunges an old character into current stakes and starts in media res too — 'Fantastic Four' would have gotten more credit for its open if 'Superman' hadn't just done it.

films.title: Apollo 13
films.note: Amanda's running gag: the launch, the light-speed docking, and the slingshot-around-a-star escape all echo Ron Howard's film, which is getting an IMAX re-release in September.

segments.heading: Summer Stars
segments.note: the season's standout performances, presented by Starbucks

films.title: The Fantastic 4: First Steps
films.note: Vanessa Kirby as the best part of the movie, and a champ on the press tour while expecting.

films.title: Thunderbolts
films.note: Florence Pugh, whom Sean says the movie can't work without, and Lewis Pullman in a dual role that puts a lot on his shoulders.

films.title: 28 Years Later
films.note: Alfie Williams, the teenage lead the whole movie — and the rest of the franchise — hangs on. Sensational.

films.title: Superman
films.note: David Corenswet, a relative unknown who wore the cape well; nailing the casting is a big reason the film worked.

films.title: Twisters
films.note: Amanda's reminder that Corenswet wasn't a total unknown before 'Superman.'

films.title: Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning
films.note: Two small parts that steal the show: Tramell Tillman, electrifying, and Katy O'Brian in the submarine segment.

films.title: Jurassic World Rebirth
films.note: Jonathan Bailey — Amanda's guy, not Sean's — petting a dinosaur with real emotion. Meant for big things.

films.title: Lilo & Stitch
films.note: Mixed on the film, sold on Sydney Agudong and Maia Kealoha, both very charming.

films.title: Final Destination Bloodlines
films.note: Amanda's favorite star of the summer: the flaw in death's design, driving one of the year's biggest box office hits.

films: I Know What You Did Last Summer

Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madn.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness

I Know What You Did Last Summer.title: I Know What You Did Last Summer
